name: Prompt Robustness Is Task-Dependent: Comparing Objective and Belief-Style Questions in LLM Evaluation
summary: arXiv:2607.05554v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Survey-style evaluations of large language models often treat a prompted response as a measure of a model's values or beliefs. This assumpt...
description: arXiv:2607.05554v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Survey-style evaluations of large language models often treat a prompted response as a measure of a model's values or beliefs. This assumption is particularly fragile when responses are read as evidence of political values, social attitudes, or beliefs. We ask whether prompt robustness differs between objective questions with fixed answe...
author: Sadia Kamal, Arefa Patwary, Anthony Marchiafava, Sagnik Ray Choudhury, Atriya Sen
